seo.title: Haystack Guardrails for Healthcare
seo.description: Compliance guide for Haystack agents in healthcare. Meet HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Healthcare teams using Haystack for RAG pipelines process sensitive PHI, where failures like hallucinations or irrelevant retrievals can lead to misdiagnoses and patient harm. Compliance with HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11, and HITECH demands auditable AI decisions and data isolation. Vex provides runtime guardrails to catch these failures before they impact care delivery.

complianceRequirements.description: Protects PHI from unauthorized disclosure; requires de-identification before LLM processing and audit trails for all access.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Monitors data flows to block unde-identified PHI from reaching LLMs, logs all pipeline decisions with tamper-proof audit trails.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates electronic records be trustworthy, auditable, and compliant with signature requirements for clinical decisions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Captures pipeline execution traces with input/output validation, ensuring AI-assisted decisions meet electronic record standards.

complianceRequirements.description: Strengthens HIPAA with breach notification and increased penalties; requires patient data isolation within processing environments.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Enforces data residency checks and alerts on exfiltration attempts, preventing PHI from leaving secure boundaries.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires demonstrated controls for security, availability, and confidentiality over 6+ months of operation.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Provides continuous compliance monitoring with failure rate dashboards and automated control evidence collection.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated Treatment Recommendations
industryRisks.description: Generator produces dangerous medical advice beyond retrieved context, leading to incorrect prescriptions.
industryRisks.example: Patient receives metformin recommendation despite documented allergy due to hallucinated dosage info.

industryRisks.name: PHI Leakage to External LLMs
industryRisks.description: Unde-identified patient data transmitted to cloud APIs, violating HIPAA data isolation rules.
industryRisks.example: Patient name and diagnosis sent to OpenAI GPT-4 during query processing.

industryRisks.name: Irrelevant Document Retrieval
industryRisks.description: Retriever returns outdated protocols or wrong patient records, causing treatment errors.
industryRisks.example: Pediatric dosage retrieved for adult patient due to embedding mismatch.

industryRisks.name: Silent Component Failures
industryRisks.description: Custom de-identification component fails without error, allowing PHI through pipeline.
industryRisks.example: NER model crashes but pipeline continues with raw clinical notes.

implementation.title: Vex Guardrail Component for PHI De-identification
implementation.description: Custom Haystack component using Vex to block pipelines if PHI detected before LLM processing.

implementation.title: Retriever Relevance Scoring with Vex
implementation.description: Validates retrieved documents meet medical relevance thresholds before generator access.

implementation.title: Hallucination Detection Post-Generator
implementation.description: Vex validates generator output stays grounded in retrieved context, blocking hallucinated responses.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Haystack in Healthcare: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
Dr. Patel's hospital deployed a Haystack RAG pipeline for antibiotic stewardship, retrieving antimicrobial guidelines and generating treatment recommendations from patient EMR data. The pipeline used a clinical embedding retriever against internal guideline documents and an OpenAI generator for natural language recommendations. During beta testing, the system recommended azithromycin for a penicillin-allergic patient because the retriever surfaced an irrelevant allergy protocol from pediatrics, and the generator hallucinated dosage instructions not present in any retrieved context.

Without compliance guardrails, this error reached the clinical interface. PHI flowed through the pipeline without de-identification checks, and no audit trail captured the retrieval-generation mismatch. The hospital faced a potential HIPAA violation when logs revealed patient names in API calls to OpenAI.
caseStudyScenario.outcome:
Vex guardrails transformed the deployment. The PHI detection component blocked 97% of unde-identified queries before LLM processing. Relevance scoring rejected the pediatric document, forcing re-retrieval of adult protocols. Hallucination detection flagged the azithromycin recommendation with 0.87 risk score, providing explainability: 'Generated dosage conflicts with all retrieved contexts.'

The pipeline achieved 99.2% compliance rate over 3 months, with full audit trails satisfying FDA 21 CFR Part 11. Dr. Patel's team gained confidence in AI-assisted decisions, reducing antibiotic misuse by 23% while maintaining SOC 2 controls.

cta.heading: Comply with Healthcare Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Add Vex guardrails to your Haystack pipelines today to prevent patient harm and HIPAA violations.
